38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only

52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
63		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
64		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed.
65		BudgetStop func() (ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
66		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
67		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points

92	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
93		mode := r.Mode
94		if mode == "" {
95			if r.Once {
96				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
97			} else {
98				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
99			}
100		}
101		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
102		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
103			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
104		}
105		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
106			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
107		}
108		return mode, idleInterval
109	}

111	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
112	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
113	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
114	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
115	
116	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
117	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
118	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
119	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
120	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
121	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
122	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
123	
124	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
125	// paths fail (3-way ort auto-merge + optional focused-resolve agent) and the
126	// bead is parked as land_conflict_unresolvable. Much shorter than
127	// MaxLoopCooldown because land conflicts often resolve quickly as sibling beads
128	// advance the base branch.
129	const LandConflictCooldown = 15 * time.Minute
130	
131	// StoreErrorCooldown is the short cooldown applied when a Store.* operation in
132	// the outcome-handling block returns a transient error. It prevents an
133	// immediate re-queue of the affected bead while allowing the loop to continue
134	// to the next candidate. Store errors during outcome handling are non-fatal:
135	// they are logged and emitted as loop-error events but do not terminate the
136	// worker.
137	const StoreErrorCooldown = 5 * time.Minute
138	
139	// ProviderUnavailableCooldown is the retry pause for route/provider outages
140	// where no configured tier can currently run the bead. This is transient
141	// scheduler state, not bead quality evidence, so it must not become a durable
142	// triage:needs-investigation park.
143	const ProviderUnavailableCooldown = 15 * time.Minute

111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)

121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}

146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}

241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}
249	
250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}
259	
260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}

64	// live on config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via RunWithConfig's

66	// runtime intent remain.

85		// lifecycle calls use profile-only routing hints sourced from fizeau.

87		ModelOverride       string

97		// MinPowerOverride, when > 0, overrides rcfg.MinPower() for this single
98		// invocation. Used by the post-merge reviewer to pin MinPower above the
99		// implementer's actual selected power, biasing routing toward a stronger
100		// reviewer model (R4 pairing: reviewer >= impl + 1 power).

109		ClearMaxPower bool

111		// calls select their own fizeau profile hints instead of inheriting the

427	// Default configuration values.
428	const (
429		DefaultHarness = "codex"
430		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
431		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
432		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
433		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
434		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
435		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
436		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
437		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
438		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
439		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
440		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
441		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
442		// ToolCallTimeout is a per-tool-call sanity cap: if a single tool_call
443		// event has no matching tool_result within this window, the drain cancels
444		// the execution. This is NOT for loop detection (see loopDetector in
445		// agent_runner_service.go) — it catches individually hung subprocesses
446		// (e.g., network deadlock in git push) that would otherwise absorb
447		// unlimited wall-clock without surfacing.
448		ToolCallTimeout = 1800000 // 30 minutes
449		DefaultLogDir   = ".ddx/agent-logs"
450	)

452	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
453	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
454	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
455	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
456	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
457		if configured == "" {
458			configured = DefaultLogDir
459		}
460		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
461			return configured
462		}
463		if projectRoot == "" {
464			return configured
465		}
466		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
467	}

82	// returns per-tier aggregates. When lastN > 0, only the most recent lastN
83	// attempts (sorted by attempt directory name, which is a sortable timestamp)
84	// are considered.
85	func computeTierSuccess(workingDir string, lastN int) ([]tierSuccessRow, error) {
86		execRoot := filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx", "executions")
87		entries, err := os.ReadDir(execRoot)
88		if err != nil {
89			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
90				return []tierSuccessRow{}, nil
91			}
92			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read executions dir: %w", err)
93		}
94	
95		names := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
96		for _, entry := range entries {
97			if entry.IsDir() {
98				names = append(names, entry.Name())
99			}
100		}
101		// Directory names are sortable timestamps (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSS-<hash>), so
102		// lexicographic sort is chronological.
103		sort.Strings(names)
104	
105		// First pass: read and keep only usable results, preserving chronological
106		// order. --last N then picks the most recent N usable attempts so that
107		// malformed or missing files never hide a valid recent attempt.
108		type loadedResult struct {
109			harness     string
110			model       string
111			outcome     string
112			failureMode string
113			costUSD     float64
114			durMS       int
115		}
116		loaded := make([]loadedResult, 0, len(names))
117		for _, name := range names {
118			resultPath := filepath.Join(execRoot, name, "result.json")
119			raw, err := os.ReadFile(resultPath)
120			if err != nil {
121				continue
122			}
123			var res agent.ExecuteBeadResult
124			if err := json.Unmarshal(raw, &res); err != nil {
125				continue
126			}
127			if res.Harness == "" {
128				continue
129			}
130			loaded = append(loaded, loadedResult{
131				harness:     res.Harness,
132				model:       res.Model,
133				outcome:     res.Outcome,
134				failureMode: res.FailureMode,
135				costUSD:     res.CostUSD,
136				durMS:       res.DurationMS,
137			})
138		}
139		if lastN > 0 && len(loaded) > lastN {
140			loaded = loaded[len(loaded)-lastN:]
141		}

---

### 4. Event Writing Flow and Attachment Model

#### **File: `cli/internal/bead/store.go`**

**AppendEvent Function (line 1022):**
- Writes BeadEvent to bead's Extra["events"] inline (for active beads) OR
- Appends to externalized sidecar at `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl` (for closed beads)
- Event body/summary capped at 512 bytes
- CreatedAt auto-set to time.Now().UTC() if zero

**Events are stored in two forms:**
1. **Inline (active beads):** `Bead.Extra["events"]` as `[]map[string]any`
2. **Sidecar (closed beads):** `.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl` (one JSON object per line)

#### **File: `cli/internal/bead/attachments.go`**

**Key Functions:**
- `readEventsAttachment(beadID)` - Reads events.jsonl, handles missing file gracefully
- `writeEventsAttachment(beadID, events)` - Writes atomically (temp + rename), creates parent dir
- `hasEventsAttachment(b)` - Checks Extra[events_attachment] for externalization reference
- `eventsAttachmentPath(beadID)` - Returns absolute path: `.ddx/attachments/<beadID>/events.jsonl`
- `eventsAttachmentRelPath(beadID)` - Returns relative path for Extra field: `<beadID>/events.jsonl`

**Attachment Storage Path:**
```
.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl
```

**Sidecar Reference:** Stored in `Bead.Extra["events_attachment"]` as relative path (e.g., `"abc123/events.jsonl"`)

---

### 5. Execution Result Writing

#### **File: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go`**

**Result and Manifest Writing:**
- `result.json` written to: `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/result.json`
- `manifest.json` written to: `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`
- Function: `writeArtifactJSON(path, payload)` - JSON marshals with indent, creates parent dirs

**Execution Bundle Files:**
```
.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/
├── result.json         # ExecuteBeadResult (final after landing/preservation)
├── manifest.json       # Execution metadata
├── prompt.md           # Agent prompt text
├── usage.json          # Token usage summary
├── diff.patch          # Generated diff (if applicable)
└── ...other artifacts
```

**WriteExecuteBeadResultArtifact()** - Rewrites result.json after landing with final orchestrator status

---

### 6. Loop Event Writing

#### **File: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`**

**ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime Structure (lines 27-90):**
```go
type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
    Log              io.Writer          // Loop progress logging
    CleanupLog       io.Writer          // Cleanup phase logging
    EventSink        io.Writer          // Loop-level events (JSON lines to stdout)
    ProgressCh       chan<- ProgressEvent
    SessionID        string
    WorkerID         string
    ProjectRoot      string
    // ... 20+ other fields
}
```

**Event Writing Patterns:**

1. **Loop-level events** (to EventSink):
   ```go
   writeLoopEvent(runtime.EventSink, runtime.SessionID, eventType, data, now().UTC())
   ```
   Format: JSON line with `{"event": "type", "payload": {...}}`

2. **Bead-level events** (to Store.AppendEvent):
   ```go
   w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, bead.BeadEvent{
       Kind:      "execute-bead-attempt",
       Summary:   "...",
       Body:      "...",
       Actor:     assignee,
       Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
       CreatedAt: at,
   })
   ```

**Event Kinds Emitted:**
- `post_attempt_decomposition.blocked` - Orchestrator-level decomposition blocked
- `triage-overflow` - Depth cap exceeded
- `review-error` - Reviewer phase error
- And others via `executeBeadLoopEvent()` which packs report status/details into a single BeadEvent

---

### 7. Existing Metrics-Related Code

#### **File: `cli/internal/cmd/agent_metrics.go`**
- `computeTierSuccess()` - Scans `.ddx/executions/*/result.json`
- Aggregates by harness/model, computes success rates
- Metrics fields: `outcome`, `harness`, `model`, `cost_usd`, `duration_ms`, `failure_mode`

#### **Directories:**
- `cli/internal/metric/` - Routing metrics (reachability, provider status)
- `cli/internal/agentmetrics/` - Agent session metrics loading
- `cli/internal/processmetrics/` - Process-level CPU/memory metrics

---

### 8. Bead Status and Constants

#### **File: `cli/internal/bead/types.go`**

**Canonical Status Values:**
- `open` - Ready for execution
- `in_progress` - Currently claimed/executing
- `closed` - Work complete
- `blocked` - External blocker
- `proposed` - Awaiting approval
- `cancelled` - Will not run

**Blocker Kinds (constants):**
- `dependency` - Unclosed dependencies
- `retry-cooldown` - Execute-loop cooldown park
- `needs-investigation` - Operator investigation required
- `operator-attention` - Manual operator action needed
- `not-execution-eligible` - Not marked for execution
- `superseded` - Made obsolete
- `epic-only` - Epic beads not execution-eligible

---

### 9. Key Integration Points for Metrics Writing

**Where to hook metrics writing:**

1. **In execute_bead_loop.go, around line 1334:**
   ```go
   if err := w.Store.AppendEvent(candidate.ID, executeBeadLoopEvent(report, assignee, now().UTC())); err != nil {
       // Could emit metrics here after event appended
   }
   ```

2. **In execute_bead.go, around line 1111:**
   ```go
   _ = writeArtifactJSON(filepath.Join(wtBundleDir, "result.json"), res)
   // Could compute and write metrics.json alongside result.json
   ```

3. **New metrics.json location:** `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/metrics.json`

4. **Event kinds to create:**
   - `kind: "metrics"` - Per-attempt metrics
   - `kind: "loop-metrics"` - Per-loop or session-level aggregates

---

### 10. Test Coverage and Examples

The codebase has extensive tests:
- `execute_bead_test.go` (116K lines)
- `execute_bead_loop_test.go` (97K lines)
- `store_test.go` (34K)
- All demonstrating actual event and result writing patterns

**Test directories:** `cli/internal/agent/testdata/` and `cli/internal/bead/testdata/`

---

## Summary

The DDx CLI uses a multi-tiered event and result storage model:

1. **Beads carry append-only event logs** via `Store.AppendEvent()` → stored inline or in sidecar attachment
2. **Executions produce result.json** in `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/`
3. **Loop emits events** via EventSink (for monitoring/stdout) and Store.AppendEvent() (for persistence)
4. **Metrics can be added** as new `metrics.json` file or new event kinds without disrupting existing flows

The flow is: **Candidate Selection → Claim → Execute → Land → AppendEvent + WriteResult → (Review) → Final Result**

35	// newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand returns "ddx work metrics backfill". It reads
36	// all beads' events.jsonl attachments and emits one row per attempt found,
37	// deduplicating against any rows already in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
38	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkMetricsBackfillCommand() *cobra.Command {
39		cmd := &cobra.Command{
40			Use:   "backfill",
41			Short: "Backfill .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl from existing bead events",
42			Long: `Scan all beads' events and produce per-attempt rows in .ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl.
43	
44	Each kind:cost event in a bead's event stream corresponds to one agent execution
45	attempt. backfill pairs each cost event with the immediately following
46	kind:execute-bead event (the finalization outcome) to produce a complete row.
47	
48	The command is idempotent: rows already present in attempts.jsonl (identified by
49	attempt_id) are skipped. Re-running backfill after a partial run or after new
50	beads are closed is safe.
51	
52	Note: backfill reads .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl and the inline
53	events in beads.jsonl. It does NOT read .ddx/executions/ directories.`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
56				projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
57				projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
58	
59				store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"))
60				allBeads, err := store.ReadAll()
61				if err != nil {
62					return fmt.Errorf("read beads: %w", err)
63				}
64	
65				inputs := make([]attemptmetrics.BeadAttemptEvents, 0, len(allBeads))
66				for _, b := range allBeads {
67					events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
68					if err != nil {
69						// Best-effort: skip beads whose events can't be read.
70						if f.WorkingDir != "" {
71							_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
72								"warning: skipping %s events: %v\n", b.ID, err)
73						}
74						continue
75					}
76					inputs = append(inputs, attemptmetrics.BeadAttemptEvents{
77						BeadID: b.ID,
78						SpecID: beadSpecID(b),
79						Events: events,
80					})
81				}
82	
83				added, err := attemptmetrics.BackfillFromEvents(projectRoot, inputs)
84				if err != nil {
85					return fmt.Errorf("backfill: %w", err)
86				}
87				_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "backfill complete: %d new rows added\n", added)
88				return nil
89			},
90		}
91		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root)")
92		return cmd
93	}

func writeExecResult(t *testing.T, execRoot, attemptID string, res map[string]any) {
	t.Helper()
	dir := filepath.Join(execRoot, attemptID)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755))
	raw, err := json.Marshal(res)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "result.json"), raw, 0o644))
}
